content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA consisting of NNN non-negative integers.

You need to perform the following operation (N‚ąí1)(N - 1)(N‚ąí1) times:


Sort the array AAA in ascending order.
Let MMM be the current size of the array AAA. Replace the array AAA by [(A2‚ąíA1),(A3‚ąíA2),‚Ä¶,(AM‚ąíAM‚ąí1)][(A_2 - A_1), (A_3 - A_2), \ldots ,(A_M - A_{M-1})][(A2‚Äč‚ąíA1‚Äč),(A3‚Äč‚ąíA2‚Äč),‚Ä¶,(AM‚Äč‚ąíAM‚ąí1‚Äč)]. Note that each time the operation is performed, the size of the array reduces by one.
Find the remaining element after (N‚ąí1)(N-1)(N‚ąí1) operations.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, output single line: the remaining element after (N‚ąí1)(N-1)(N‚ąí1) operations.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: The operations look like:

Operation 111: Sort the array. Now, AAA becomes [0,3,19,29][0, 3, 19, 29][0,3,19,29]. Replace the array. Thus, A=[(3‚ąí0),(19‚ąí3),(29‚ąí19)]=[3,16,10]A = [(3-0), (19-3), (29-19)] = [3, 16, 10]A=[(3‚ąí0),(19‚ąí3),(29‚ąí19)]=[3,16,10].
Operation 222: Sort the array. Now, AAA becomes [3,10,16][3, 10, 16][3,10,16]. Replace the array. Thus, A=[(10‚ąí3),(16‚ąí10)]=[7,6]A = [(10-3), (16-10)] = [7, 6]A=[(10‚ąí3),(16‚ąí10)]=[7,6].
Operation 333: Sort the array. Now, AAA becomes [6,7][6, 7][6,7]. Replace the array. Thus, A=[(7‚ąí6)]=[1]A = [(7-6)] = [1]A=[(7‚ąí6)]=[1].



Thus, the remaining element after 333 operations is 111.

Test case 2: Since N=1N = 1N=1, you don't need to perform any operation. The remaining element after 000 operations is 777777777.
